spec_match.rationale: The demo clearly contrasts Chrome 152 partitioned storage keys with the legacy unpartitioned access shape requestStorageAccessFor enabled.

interactive.rationale: Clickable model tabs and a refreshable live support readout update the page without requiring the removed API to exist.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept matches the parent blurb by toggling between isolated partition keys and the pre-removal cross-site sharing model.

edge_cases.rationale: The live status panel handles browsers where requestStorageAccessFor is absent, still exposed, or where replacement Storage Access methods are unavailable.

references.rationale: The see-also section now includes the required chromestatus.com/feature/5162221567082496 link.

design_system.rationale: The added controls and status cards use existing CSS variables, and the local button background also sets its text color.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: The visualizer now satisfies the hard reference invariant and remains useful in browsers where requestStorageAccessFor has been removed by detecting support, avoiding removed calls, and keeping the storage-model comparison interactive.
